pyensemblefs
  • API Reference
  • Aggregators
  • View page source

Aggregators

The pyensemblefs.aggregators subpackage implements multiple families of aggregation methods that operate on bootstrap outputs (scores, ranks, or binary supports). These correspond to the score-based, rank-based, and subset-based rules described in the paper.

Overview

All aggregators follow a common contract defined in pyensemblefs.aggregators.base.BaseAggregator:

  • Input is a 2D array results of shape (n_bootstraps, n_features).

  • aggregate() returns a 1D vector of length n_features (scores, ranks or a binary mask, depending on the subclass).

  • fit() computes an internal ranking and a selection mask based on top_k and the aggregated values.

Aggregator families

Score-based aggregators

Defined in pyensemblefs.aggregators.score, they treat higher values as better and work directly on real-valued matrices of scores (e.g., feature importances or relevance scores per bootstrap):

  • pyensemblefs.aggregators.score.MeanAggregator Arithmetic mean of scores across bootstraps.

  • pyensemblefs.aggregators.score.SumAggregator Sum of scores across bootstraps.

  • pyensemblefs.aggregators.score.MedianAggregator Median of scores (robust to outliers).

  • pyensemblefs.aggregators.score.TrimmedMeanAggregator Alpha-trimmed mean, removing a fraction of lowest and highest scores.

  • pyensemblefs.aggregators.score.WinsorizedMeanAggregator Winsorized mean, clipping extremes to alpha-quantiles.

  • pyensemblefs.aggregators.score.GeometricMeanAggregator Geometric mean, emphasizing consistently high scores.

  • pyensemblefs.aggregators.score.RankProductAggregator Rank-product style aggregation on scores (log-domain), returning -log-product so that higher values indicate more stable high scores.

  • pyensemblefs.aggregators.score.WeightedScoreAggregator Weighted average of scores across bootstraps, using user-provided weights.

  • pyensemblefs.aggregators.score.BordaFromScoresAggregator Converts per-bootstrap scores to ranks and applies Borda count, summing Borda points across bootstraps.

  • pyensemblefs.aggregators.score.SelectionFrequencyAggregator Converts binary supports into selection frequencies in [0, 1]; useful when base selectors return subsets instead of scores.

These aggregators directly implement the score-based pooling and selection-frequency strategies summarized in the Aggregation Methods section of the paper.

Rank-based aggregators

Defined in pyensemblefs.aggregators.rank, they operate on score matrices but internally convert them to ranks per bootstrap (1 = best) and then combine these ranks. They inherit from pyensemblefs.aggregators.base.RankAggregator, where lower values are better:

  • pyensemblefs.aggregators.rank.MeanRankAggregator Mean rank across bootstraps.

  • pyensemblefs.aggregators.rank.MedianRankAggregator Median rank across bootstraps.

  • pyensemblefs.aggregators.rank.ConsensusRankAggregator Conceptually equivalent to mean rank, kept for semantic clarity.

  • pyensemblefs.aggregators.rank.BordaFromRanksAggregator Borda fusion from rank input; if the input does not look like proper ranks, it is automatically converted from scores.

  • pyensemblefs.aggregators.rank.TrimmedMeanRankAggregator Alpha-trimmed mean of ranks, discarding extreme ranks before averaging.

  • pyensemblefs.aggregators.rank.WinsorizedMeanRankAggregator Winsorized mean of ranks, clipping extreme ranks to alpha-quantiles.

  • pyensemblefs.aggregators.rank.GeometricMeanRankAggregator Geometric mean of ranks, emphasizing consistently high positions.

  • pyensemblefs.aggregators.rank.RankProductAggregator Rank-product aggregation (log-domain), where lower values correspond to features consistently near the top.

These correspond to the rank-based consensus family discussed in the paper.

Subset-based (voting) aggregators

Defined in pyensemblefs.aggregators.subset, they assume that the bootstrap outputs are binary supports (0/1), and aggregate them using frequency and voting rules. They inherit from pyensemblefs.aggregators.base.BinaryAggregator and ultimately produce a binary mask of selected features:

  • pyensemblefs.aggregators.subset.MajorityVoteAggregator Uses selection frequencies and applies a majority threshold (e.g. 0.5); optionally enforces a maximum top_k by keeping the most frequent features.

  • pyensemblefs.aggregators.subset.TopKBinaryAggregator Selects exactly top_k features with highest selection frequency.

  • pyensemblefs.aggregators.subset.ThresholdAggregator Selects all features whose frequency is above a fixed threshold τ.

  • pyensemblefs.aggregators.subset.QuantileThresholdAggregator Selects features whose selection frequency is above a given quantile.

  • pyensemblefs.aggregators.subset.WeightedMajorityVoteAggregator Weighted voting over bootstrap supports; each bootstrap can have a different weight.

  • pyensemblefs.aggregators.subset.FDRControlledFrequencyAggregator Uses a binomial model and Benjamini–Hochberg FDR control to select features with statistically significant selection frequency.

  • pyensemblefs.aggregators.subset.ClopperPearsonCIThresholdAggregator Uses Clopper–Pearson confidence intervals on selection frequency to retain features whose lower CI bound exceeds a threshold.

These classes implement the subset-based voting and FDR/CI-based frequency rules described in the Aggregation Methods section of the paper.

Core classes

Module reference

Base classes

class pyensemblefs.aggregators.base.BaseAggregator(top_k=None)[source]

Bases: ABC

Base class for aggregating feature selection results.

Contract:

  • aggregate(results) -> 1D array (n_features,)

  • Input ‘results’ must be 2D: (n_bootstraps, n_features)

  • By default (ScoreAggregators), larger values are better.

  • Subclasses may override fit() to invert the criterion (e.g., RankAggregator).

Parameters:

top_k (Optional[int])

abstractmethod aggregate(results)[source]
Parameters:

results (np.ndarray of shape (n_bootstraps, n_features)) – Per-bootstrap results to aggregate.

Returns:

aggregated – Aggregated vector (scores or ranks).

Return type:

np.ndarray of shape (n_features,)

fit(results)[source]

Default fit: assumes higher aggregated values are better (score-like).

Parameters:

results (ndarray)

get_support(indices=False)[source]

Scikit-learn-like support API. - If indices=False: returns boolean mask (n_features,) - If indices=True : returns selected indices (1D)

Parameters:

indices (bool)

property rank_: ndarray | None

Return indices of features ordered best→worst. This is an ordered list of indices, NOT per-feature rank numbers.

property scores_: ndarray | None

Return raw aggregated values (scores or ranks depending on subclass).

set_top_k(top_k)[source]

Set or update top_k after construction.

Return type:

BaseAggregator

Parameters:

top_k (int | None)

class pyensemblefs.aggregators.base.BinaryAggregator(top_k=None)[source]

Bases: BaseAggregator

Aggregators that produce a binary selection mask (0/1). aggregate() must return a 0/1 vector; fit() stores it and builds a stable order.

Parameters:

top_k (Optional[int])

fit(results)[source]

Default fit: assumes higher aggregated values are better (score-like).

Parameters:

results (ndarray)

class pyensemblefs.aggregators.base.RankAggregator(top_k=None)[source]

Bases: BaseAggregator

Aggregators producing per-feature rank values, where LOWER = better. aggregate() must return per-feature rank values (e.g., mean rank).

Parameters:

top_k (Optional[int])

fit(results)[source]

Default fit: assumes higher aggregated values are better (score-like).

Parameters:

results (ndarray)

class pyensemblefs.aggregators.base.ScoreAggregator(top_k=None)[source]

Bases: BaseAggregator

Aggregators where higher score = better (use BaseAggregator.fit).

Parameters:

top_k (Optional[int])

Score-based aggregators

class pyensemblefs.aggregators.score.BordaFromScoresAggregator(top_k=None)[source]

Bases: ScoreAggregator

Borda aggregation from per-bootstrap scores:

  1. convert each bootstrap row to ranks (1 = best),

  2. assign Borda points (n_features - rank),

  3. sum points across bootstraps (higher = better).

Parameters:

top_k (Optional[int])

aggregate(results)[source]
Parameters:

results (np.ndarray of shape (n_bootstraps, n_features)) – Per-bootstrap results to aggregate.

Returns:

aggregated – Aggregated vector (scores or ranks).

Return type:

np.ndarray of shape (n_features,)

class pyensemblefs.aggregators.score.GeometricMeanAggregator(eps=1e-12, top_k=None)[source]

Bases: ScoreAggregator

Geometric mean across bootstraps: exp( mean( log(max(scores, eps)) ) ), emphasizing consistent high scores.

Parameters:

  • eps (float)

  • top_k (Optional[int])

aggregate(results)[source]
Parameters:

results (np.ndarray of shape (n_bootstraps, n_features)) – Per-bootstrap results to aggregate.

Returns:

aggregated – Aggregated vector (scores or ranks).

Return type:

np.ndarray of shape (n_features,)

class pyensemblefs.aggregators.score.MeanAggregator(top_k=None)[source]

Bases: ScoreAggregator

Arithmetic mean of per-bootstrap scores (higher = better).

Parameters:

top_k (Optional[int])

aggregate(results)[source]
Parameters:

results (np.ndarray of shape (n_bootstraps, n_features)) – Per-bootstrap results to aggregate.

Returns:

aggregated – Aggregated vector (scores or ranks).

Return type:

np.ndarray of shape (n_features,)

class pyensemblefs.aggregators.score.MedianAggregator(top_k=None)[source]

Bases: ScoreAggregator

Median of per-bootstrap scores (robust to outliers).

Parameters:

top_k (Optional[int])

aggregate(results)[source]
Parameters:

results (np.ndarray of shape (n_bootstraps, n_features)) – Per-bootstrap results to aggregate.

Returns:

aggregated – Aggregated vector (scores or ranks).

Return type:

np.ndarray of shape (n_features,)

class pyensemblefs.aggregators.score.RankProductAggregator(eps=1e-12, top_k=None)[source]

Bases: ScoreAggregator

Rank-product style aggregator for scores: compute the product in log-domain. Lower log-product indicates consistently high scores; we return the negative log-product so that higher is better for downstream selection.

Returns:

-sum(log(max(scores, eps))) across bootstraps (higher = better).

Return type:

np.ndarray

Parameters:

  • eps (float)

  • top_k (Optional[int])

aggregate(results)[source]
Parameters:

results (np.ndarray of shape (n_bootstraps, n_features)) – Per-bootstrap results to aggregate.

Returns:

aggregated – Aggregated vector (scores or ranks).

Return type:

np.ndarray of shape (n_features,)

class pyensemblefs.aggregators.score.SelectionFrequencyAggregator(top_k=None)[source]

Bases: ScoreAggregator

Convert binary 0/1 supports into selection frequencies in [0, 1]. Accepts boolean, {0,1} int/float; values are cast to {0,1}. Use this when the base FS method returns subsets (supports).

Parameters:

top_k (Optional[int])

aggregate(results)[source]
Parameters:

results (np.ndarray of shape (n_bootstraps, n_features)) – Per-bootstrap results to aggregate.

Returns:

aggregated – Aggregated vector (scores or ranks).

Return type:

np.ndarray of shape (n_features,)

class pyensemblefs.aggregators.score.SumAggregator(top_k=None)[source]

Bases: ScoreAggregator

Sum of per-bootstrap scores (higher = better).

Parameters:

top_k (Optional[int])

aggregate(results)[source]
Parameters:

results (np.ndarray of shape (n_bootstraps, n_features)) – Per-bootstrap results to aggregate.

Returns:

aggregated – Aggregated vector (scores or ranks).

Return type:

np.ndarray of shape (n_features,)

class pyensemblefs.aggregators.score.TrimmedMeanAggregator(alpha=0.1, top_k=None)[source]

Bases: ScoreAggregator

Alpha-trimmed mean across bootstraps: trims a fraction alpha on each tail before averaging. Useful to mitigate outliers at the bootstrap level.

Parameters:

  • alpha (float in [0, 0.5)) – Proportion to trim on each tail (e.g., 0.1 trims 10% lowest & 10% highest).

  • top_k (Optional[int])

aggregate(results)[source]
Parameters:

results (np.ndarray of shape (n_bootstraps, n_features)) – Per-bootstrap results to aggregate.

Returns:

aggregated – Aggregated vector (scores or ranks).

Return type:

np.ndarray of shape (n_features,)

class pyensemblefs.aggregators.score.WeightedScoreAggregator(weights=None, top_k=None)[source]

Bases: ScoreAggregator

Weighted average of per-bootstrap scores. If weights is None, reduces to the arithmetic mean.

Parameters:

  • weights (array-like of shape (n_bootstraps,), optional) – Non-negative weights. They will be normalized to sum to 1.

  • top_k (Optional[int])

aggregate(results)[source]
Parameters:

results (np.ndarray of shape (n_bootstraps, n_features)) – Per-bootstrap results to aggregate.

Returns:

aggregated – Aggregated vector (scores or ranks).

Return type:

np.ndarray of shape (n_features,)

class pyensemblefs.aggregators.score.WinsorizedMeanAggregator(alpha=0.1, top_k=None)[source]

Bases: ScoreAggregator

Winsorized mean across bootstraps: clip extremes to alpha-quantiles before averaging.

Parameters:

  • alpha (float in [0, 0.5)) – Proportion to winsorize on each tail.

  • top_k (Optional[int])

aggregate(results)[source]
Parameters:

results (np.ndarray of shape (n_bootstraps, n_features)) – Per-bootstrap results to aggregate.

Returns:

aggregated – Aggregated vector (scores or ranks).

Return type:

np.ndarray of shape (n_features,)

Rank-based aggregators

class pyensemblefs.aggregators.rank.BordaFromRanksAggregator(top_k=None)[source]

Bases: ScoreAggregator

Borda aggregation from RANK INPUT.

Behavior:

  • If input is integer ranks in {1, …, p}, use them as-is.

  • If input looks like raw scores (floats) or ranks outside [1, p], automatically convert to integer ranks (1=best) using _scores_to_ranks_per_bootstrap.

Output:

  • Borda points (higher = better), hence this class derives from ScoreAggregator.

Parameters:

top_k (Optional[int])

aggregate(results)[source]
Parameters:

results (np.ndarray of shape (n_bootstraps, n_features)) – Per-bootstrap results to aggregate.

Returns:

aggregated – Aggregated vector (scores or ranks).

Return type:

np.ndarray of shape (n_features,)

class pyensemblefs.aggregators.rank.ConsensusRankAggregator(top_k=None)[source]

Bases: RankAggregator

Generic consensus rank by averaging ranks (same as MeanRank but kept for semantic clarity and potential future changes).

Parameters:

top_k (Optional[int])

aggregate(results)[source]
Parameters:

results (np.ndarray of shape (n_bootstraps, n_features)) – Per-bootstrap results to aggregate.

Returns:

aggregated – Aggregated vector (scores or ranks).

Return type:

np.ndarray of shape (n_features,)

class pyensemblefs.aggregators.rank.GeometricMeanRankAggregator(top_k=None)[source]

Bases: RankAggregator

Aggregate per-bootstrap ranks via geometric mean: exp(mean(log(rank))) — emphasizes consistently high positions.

Parameters:

top_k (Optional[int])

aggregate(results)[source]
Parameters:

results (np.ndarray of shape (n_bootstraps, n_features)) – Per-bootstrap results to aggregate.

Returns:

aggregated – Aggregated vector (scores or ranks).

Return type:

np.ndarray of shape (n_features,)

class pyensemblefs.aggregators.rank.MeanRankAggregator(top_k=None)[source]

Bases: RankAggregator

Aggregate by the mean rank across bootstraps (lower = better).

Parameters:

top_k (Optional[int])

aggregate(results)[source]
Parameters:

results (np.ndarray of shape (n_bootstraps, n_features)) – Per-bootstrap results to aggregate.

Returns:

aggregated – Aggregated vector (scores or ranks).

Return type:

np.ndarray of shape (n_features,)

class pyensemblefs.aggregators.rank.MedianRankAggregator(top_k=None)[source]

Bases: RankAggregator

Aggregate by the median rank across bootstraps (robust; lower = better).

Parameters:

top_k (Optional[int])

aggregate(results)[source]
Parameters:

results (np.ndarray of shape (n_bootstraps, n_features)) – Per-bootstrap results to aggregate.

Returns:

aggregated – Aggregated vector (scores or ranks).

Return type:

np.ndarray of shape (n_features,)

class pyensemblefs.aggregators.rank.RankProductAggregator(top_k=None)[source]

Bases: RankAggregator

Aggregate via rank-product across bootstraps (in log domain for stability). Sensitive to features consistently near the top.

Returns the sum of log-ranks; lower is better.

Parameters:

top_k (Optional[int])

aggregate(results)[source]
Parameters:

results (np.ndarray of shape (n_bootstraps, n_features)) – Per-bootstrap results to aggregate.

Returns:

aggregated – Aggregated vector (scores or ranks).

Return type:

np.ndarray of shape (n_features,)

class pyensemblefs.aggregators.rank.TrimmedMeanRankAggregator(top_k=None, alpha=0.1)[source]

Bases: RankAggregator

Aggregate per-bootstrap ranks using an alpha-trimmed mean. Trims a fraction ‘alpha’ on both tails before averaging.

Parameters:

alpha (float in [0, 0.5)) – Proportion to trim on each tail (e.g., 0.1 trims 10% lowest and 10% highest).

aggregate(results)[source]
Parameters:

results (np.ndarray of shape (n_bootstraps, n_features)) – Per-bootstrap results to aggregate.

Returns:

aggregated – Aggregated vector (scores or ranks).

Return type:

np.ndarray of shape (n_features,)

class pyensemblefs.aggregators.rank.WinsorizedMeanRankAggregator(top_k=None, alpha=0.1)[source]

Bases: RankAggregator

Aggregate per-bootstrap ranks using a winsorized mean: extreme ranks are clipped to alpha-quantiles before averaging.

Parameters:

alpha (float in [0, 0.5)) – Proportion to winsorize on each tail.

aggregate(results)[source]
Parameters:

results (np.ndarray of shape (n_bootstraps, n_features)) – Per-bootstrap results to aggregate.

Returns:

aggregated – Aggregated vector (scores or ranks).

Return type:

np.ndarray of shape (n_features,)

Subset / voting aggregators

class pyensemblefs.aggregators.subset.ClopperPearsonCIThresholdAggregator(alpha_ci=0.05, t=0.5, top_k=None)[source]

Bases: BinaryAggregator

Parameters:

  • alpha_ci (float)

  • t (float)

  • top_k (int | None)

aggregate(results)[source]
Parameters:

results (np.ndarray of shape (n_bootstraps, n_features)) – Per-bootstrap results to aggregate.

Returns:

aggregated – Aggregated vector (scores or ranks).

Return type:

np.ndarray of shape (n_features,)

fit(results)[source]

Default fit: assumes higher aggregated values are better (score-like).

Parameters:

results (ndarray)

fit(results)[source]

results (ndarray)
